---
title: "UI_P004: Component N-tuple Pattern Principle"
subtitle: "Extend component architecture beyond triple pattern for flexible componentization"
chapter: "CH04"
category: "principle"
number: "UI_P004"
date-created: "2025-04-02"
date-modified: "2025-08-17"
author: "Claude"
type: "principle"
law: "UI Components Law"
article-number: "Article 4"
derives_from:
  - "CH00.MP017: Separation of Concerns"
related_to:
  - "UI_R001: UI-Server-Defaults N-tuple"
  - "UI_P003: Sidebar Filtering Only"
  - "UI_R011: Hybrid Sidebar Pattern"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Article Overview {#overview}

**UI Components Law Article 4 (Component N-tuple Pattern Principle)**

This article establishes that component architecture should follow an n-tuple pattern where each logical component is split into separate, specialized files based on responsibility, with each file focused on a single aspect of component functionality.

:::{.callout-important}
## Core Principle
Components are organized as n-tuples of specialized files, extending beyond the basic UI-Server-Defaults triple to accommodate additional aspects like Filters, Utils, Validators, Tests, and Documentation as needed, with each file maintaining single responsibility.
:::

# Section 1: Rationale {#rationale}

## Subsection 1: Evolution from Triple Pattern {#evolution}

This principle extends the traditional UI-Server-Defaults triple pattern to accommodate additional component aspects as needed:

1. **Enhanced Separation of Concerns**: Each component aspect has a dedicated file with a single responsibility
2. **Flexible Componentization**: New component aspects can be added without restructuring existing code
3. **Improved Maintainability**: Changes to one aspect don't require modifying files for other aspects
4. **Clearer Organization**: File naming clearly indicates each file's responsibility
5. **Better Testability**: Each component aspect can be tested independently

## Subsection 2: Benefits {#benefits}

第一項：開發效率 (Development efficiency)

- Parallel development of different aspects
- Reduced merge conflicts in version control
- Clear ownership of component parts
- Focused code reviews
- Simplified debugging

第二項：架構彈性 (Architectural flexibility)

- Progressive enhancement from simple to complex
- Optional aspects for specialized needs
- Reusable aspect patterns
- Scalable component growth
- Clean extension points

# Section 2: Core Component Aspects {#core-aspects}

## Subsection 1: Basic Triple (Required) {#basic-triple}

第一項：UI 元件 (UI component)

```r
# customerProfileUI.R
customerProfileUI <- function(id, defaults = customerProfileDefaults()) {
  ns <- NS(id)
  
  tagList(
    # Component structure
    box(
      title = defaults$title,
      status = defaults$status,
      solidHeader = defaults$solidHeader,
      width = defaults$width,
      
      # Input elements
      selectInput(
        ns("customer_id"),
        label = "Select Customer",
        choices = NULL,
        selected = defaults$selected_customer
      ),
      
      # Output elements
      valueBoxOutput(ns("total_value")),
      plotOutput(ns("history_chart")),
      DTOutput(ns("details_table"))
    )
  )
}
```

第二項：伺服器邏輯 (Server logic)

```r
# customerProfileServer.R
customerProfileServer <- function(id, 
                                   data = reactive(NULL),
                                   options = customerProfileDefaults()) {
  moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {
    
    # Reactive data processing
    customer_data <- reactive({
      req(data())
      req(input$customer_id)
      
      data() %>%
        filter(customer_id == input$customer_id)
    })
    
    # Value box output
    output$total_value <- renderValueBox({
      valueBox(
        value = sum(customer_data()$value, na.rm = TRUE),
        subtitle = "Total Value",
        icon = icon("dollar-sign"),
        color = options$value_box_color
      )
    })
    
    # Chart output
    output$history_chart <- renderPlot({
      plot_customer_history(
        customer_data(),
        options$chart_options
      )
    })
    
    # Table output
    output$details_table <- renderDT({
      datatable(
        customer_data(),
        options = options$table_options
      )
    })
    
    # Return reactive values for other modules
    return(list(
      selected_customer = reactive(input$customer_id),
      customer_data = customer_data
    ))
  })
}
```

第三項：預設值配置 (Defaults configuration)

```r
# customerProfileDefaults.R
customerProfileDefaults <- function() {
  list(
    # UI defaults
    title = "Customer Profile",
    status = "primary",
    solidHeader = TRUE,
    width = 12,
    selected_customer = NULL,
    
    # Display options
    value_box_color = "blue",
    
    # Chart options
    chart_options = list(
      type = "line",
      color_scheme = "Set1",
      show_legend = TRUE,
      date_format = "%Y-%m-%d"
    ),
    
    # Table options
    table_options = list(
      pageLength = 10,
      searching = TRUE,
      ordering = TRUE,
      responsive = TRUE
    ),
    
    # Data options
    max_records = 1000,
    date_range_default = 90  # days
  )
}
```

# Section 3: Extended Component Aspects {#extended-aspects}

## Subsection 1: Filtering Aspect {#filters}

第一項：組件專用篩選 (Component-specific filters)

```r
# customerProfileFilters.R
customerProfileFilters <- function(id, defaults = customerProfileDefaults()) {
  ns <- NS(id)
  
  tagList(
    h4("Customer Filters"),
    
    # Customer type filter
    selectInput(
      ns("customer_type"),
      "Customer Type",
      choices = c("All", "Active", "Inactive", "New"),
      selected = "All"
    ),
    
    # Date range filter
    dateRangeInput(
      ns("date_range"),
      "Transaction Period",
      start = Sys.Date() - defaults$date_range_default,
      end = Sys.Date()
    ),
    
    # Value range filter
    sliderInput(
      ns("value_range"),
      "Transaction Value Range",
      min = 0,
      max = 10000,
      value = c(0, 10000),
      step = 100
    ),
    
    # Category filter
    checkboxGroupInput(
      ns("categories"),
      "Product Categories",
      choices = c("Electronics", "Clothing", "Food", "Other"),
      selected = c("Electronics", "Clothing", "Food", "Other")
    ),
    
    # Filter actions
    actionButton(
      ns("apply_filters"),
      "Apply Filters",
      icon = icon("filter"),
      class = "btn-primary btn-block"
    ),
    
    actionButton(
      ns("reset_filters"),
      "Reset",
      icon = icon("undo"),
      class = "btn-secondary btn-block"
    )
  )
}
```

## Subsection 2: Utilities Aspect {#utils}

第一項：輔助函數 (Helper functions)

```r
# customerProfileUtils.R
# Component-specific utility functions

#' Plot customer purchase history
plot_customer_history <- function(data, options = list()) {
  req(nrow(data) > 0)
  
  # Prepare data
  plot_data <- data %>%
    group_by(date) %>%
    summarise(total = sum(value, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
    arrange(date)
  
  # Create plot based on type
  if (options$type == "line") {
    p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = date, y = total)) +
      geom_line(color = "steelblue", size = 1.2) +
      geom_point(color = "steelblue", size = 3)
  } else if (options$type == "bar") {
    p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = date, y = total)) +
      geom_col(fill = "steelblue")
  }
  
  # Apply theme and formatting
  p <- p +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(
      title = "Purchase History",
      x = "Date",
      y = "Total Value"
    ) +
    scale_x_date(date_labels = options$date_format) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::dollar)
  
  if (options$show_legend) {
    p <- p + theme(legend.position = "bottom")
  }
  
  return(p)
}

#' Calculate customer metrics
calculate_customer_metrics <- function(data) {
  metrics <- list(
    total_purchases = nrow(data),
    total_value = sum(data$value, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_value = mean(data$value, na.rm = TRUE),
    first_purchase = min(data$date, na.rm = TRUE),
    last_purchase = max(data$date, na.rm = TRUE),
    active_days = length(unique(data$date))
  )
  
  # Calculate RFM scores
  metrics$recency <- as.numeric(Sys.Date() - metrics$last_purchase)
  metrics$frequency <- metrics$total_purchases
  metrics$monetary <- metrics$total_value
  
  return(metrics)
}

#' Format customer data for display
format_customer_data <- function(data) {
  data %>%
    mutate(
      value = scales::dollar(value),
      date = format(date, "%Y-%m-%d"),
      status = factor(status, levels = c("Pending", "Complete", "Cancelled"))
    ) %>%
    select(date, product, category, value, status)
}
```

## Subsection 3: Validators Aspect {#validators}

第一項：輸入驗證 (Input validation)

```r
# customerProfileValidators.R
# Input validation functions

#' Validate customer selection
validate_customer_selection <- function(customer_id, available_customers) {
  if (is.null(customer_id) || customer_id == "") {
    return(list(
      valid = FALSE,
      message = "Please select a customer"
    ))
  }
  
  if (!customer_id %in% available_customers) {
    return(list(
      valid = FALSE,
      message = "Selected customer not found"
    ))
  }
  
  return(list(valid = TRUE))
}

#' Validate date range
validate_date_range <- function(date_range) {
  if (is.null(date_range) || length(date_range) != 2) {
    return(list(
      valid = FALSE,
      message = "Invalid date range"
    ))
  }
  
  if (date_range[1] > date_range[2]) {
    return(list(
      valid = FALSE,
      message = "Start date must be before end date"
    ))
  }
  
  if (date_range[2] > Sys.Date()) {
    return(list(
      valid = FALSE,
      message = "End date cannot be in the future"
    ))
  }
  
  return(list(valid = TRUE))
}

#' Validate data completeness
validate_customer_data <- function(data) {
  required_columns <- c("customer_id", "date", "value", "product")
  
  missing_columns <- setdiff(required_columns, names(data))
  if (length(missing_columns) > 0) {
    return(list(
      valid = FALSE,
      message = paste("Missing required columns:", 
                      paste(missing_columns, collapse = ", "))
    ))
  }
  
  if (nrow(data) == 0) {
    return(list(
      valid = FALSE,
      message = "No data available for selected customer"
    ))
  }
  
  return(list(valid = TRUE))
}
```

# Section 4: Testing and Documentation Aspects {#testing-docs}

## Subsection 1: Tests Aspect {#tests}

```r
# customerProfileTests.R
# Component test suite

test_that("customerProfileUI creates correct structure", {
  ui <- customerProfileUI("test")
  
  expect_true(inherits(ui, "shiny.tag.list"))
  expect_true(grepl("test-customer_id", as.character(ui)))
  expect_true(grepl("test-total_value", as.character(ui)))
})

test_that("customerProfileServer processes data correctly", {
  testServer(
    customerProfileServer,
    args = list(
      data = reactive(test_customer_data),
      options = customerProfileDefaults()
    ),
    {
      session$setInputs(customer_id = "CUST001")
      
      expect_equal(nrow(customer_data()), 10)
      expect_true(all(customer_data()$customer_id == "CUST001"))
    }
  )
})

test_that("Validators work correctly", {
  # Test customer validation
  result <- validate_customer_selection("CUST001", c("CUST001", "CUST002"))
  expect_true(result$valid)
  
  result <- validate_customer_selection("CUST999", c("CUST001", "CUST002"))
  expect_false(result$valid)
  
  # Test date range validation
  result <- validate_date_range(c(Sys.Date() - 30, Sys.Date()))
  expect_true(result$valid)
  
  result <- validate_date_range(c(Sys.Date(), Sys.Date() - 30))
  expect_false(result$valid)
})
```

## Subsection 2: Documentation Aspect {#documentation}

```markdown
# customerProfileDocs.md

## Customer Profile Component

### Overview
The Customer Profile component displays comprehensive information about a selected customer including purchase history, metrics, and detailed transaction records.

### Usage
```r
# Basic usage
ui <- customerProfileUI("profile1")
server <- function(input, output, session) {
  customerProfileServer("profile1", data = customer_data)
}
```

### Parameters
- `id`: Module namespace ID
- `data`: Reactive data source containing customer transactions
- `options`: Configuration options (see customerProfileDefaults)

### Outputs
The server function returns:
- `selected_customer`: Currently selected customer ID
- `customer_data`: Filtered data for the selected customer

### Dependencies
- bs4Dash for UI components
- DT for data tables
- ggplot2 for charts
```

# Section 5: Implementation Patterns {#implementation}

## Subsection 1: Directory Structure {#directory}

```
10_rshinyapp_components/
├── customer_profile/
│   ├── customerProfileUI.R
│   ├── customerProfileServer.R
│   ├── customerProfileDefaults.R
│   ├── customerProfileFilters.R
│   ├── customerProfileUtils.R
│   ├── customerProfileValidators.R
│   ├── customerProfileTests.R
│   └── customerProfileDocs.md
├── sales_dashboard/
│   ├── salesDashboardUI.R
│   ├── salesDashboardServer.R
│   └── salesDashboardDefaults.R  # Minimal component
└── product_catalog/
    ├── productCatalogUI.R
    ├── productCatalogServer.R
    ├── productCatalogDefaults.R
    ├── productCatalogFilters.R
    └── productCatalogUtils.R  # Extended component
```

## Subsection 2: Component Registration {#registration}

第一項：註冊函數 (Registration function)

```r
# Component registration and loading
register_component <- function(component_name, component_dir) {
  # Build file paths
  build_path <- function(suffix) {
    file.path(component_dir, paste0(component_name, suffix, ".R"))
  }
  
  # Required aspects (must exist)
  required <- c("UI", "Server", "Defaults")
  for (aspect in required) {
    path <- build_path(aspect)
    if (!file.exists(path)) {
      stop(sprintf("Required component file missing: %s", path))
    }
  }
  
  # Load required aspects
  ui <- source(build_path("UI"))$value
  server <- source(build_path("Server"))$value
  defaults <- source(build_path("Defaults"))$value
  
  # Optional aspects
  optional_aspects <- c("Filters", "Utils", "Validators", "Tests")
  loaded_aspects <- list(
    ui = ui,
    server = server,
    defaults = defaults
  )
  
  for (aspect in optional_aspects) {
    path <- build_path(aspect)
    if (file.exists(path)) {
      loaded_aspects[[tolower(aspect)]] <- source(path)$value
      message(sprintf("Loaded optional aspect: %s", aspect))
    }
  }
  
  # Return component bundle
  class(loaded_aspects) <- c("shiny_component", "list")
  return(loaded_aspects)
}

# Usage
customer_profile <- register_component(
  "customerProfile",
  "10_rshinyapp_components/customer_profile"
)
```

# Section 6: Best Practices {#best-practices}

## Subsection 1: Progressive Enhancement {#progressive}

```r
# Start with basic triple
minimal_component/
├── minimalUI.R
├── minimalServer.R
└── minimalDefaults.R

# Add aspects as complexity grows
enhanced_component/
├── enhancedUI.R
├── enhancedServer.R
├── enhancedDefaults.R
├── enhancedFilters.R      # Added when filtering needed
└── enhancedUtils.R         # Added when logic extracted

# Full-featured component
complete_component/
├── completeUI.R
├── completeServer.R
├── completeDefaults.R
├── completeFilters.R
├── completeUtils.R
├── completeValidators.R
├── completeTests.R
└── completeDocs.md
```

## Subsection 2: Naming Conventions {#naming}

```r
# Consistent naming patterns
# Pattern: componentNameAspect.R

# CamelCase (recommended)
customerProfileUI.R
customerProfileServer.R
customerProfileDefaults.R

# Alternative: snake_case
customer_profile_ui.R
customer_profile_server.R
customer_profile_defaults.R

# Avoid mixing styles in same component
# Bad: customerProfile_ui.R (mixed case)
# Bad: CustomerProfileUi.R (incorrect capitalization)
```

# Implementation Checklist {#checklist}

:::{.callout-note}
## N-tuple Pattern Checklist

**Required Aspects:**
- [ ] UI file with visual structure
- [ ] Server file with business logic
- [ ] Defaults file with configuration

**Optional Aspects:**
- [ ] Filters for component-specific filtering
- [ ] Utils for helper functions
- [ ] Validators for input validation
- [ ] Tests for component testing
- [ ] Documentation for usage guide

**Organization:**
- [ ] Component folder created
- [ ] Consistent naming convention
- [ ] Single responsibility per file
- [ ] Clear aspect separation

**Implementation:**
- [ ] Registration function available
- [ ] Component loading tested
- [ ] Optional aspects handled gracefully
- [ ] Dependencies documented

**Quality:**
- [ ] Each aspect independently testable
- [ ] No cross-aspect dependencies
- [ ] Clear interfaces between aspects
- [ ] Progressive enhancement supported
:::

# Conclusion {#conclusion}

The Component N-tuple Pattern Principle provides a flexible and scalable architecture for Shiny components. By extending beyond the basic triple pattern, components can grow in complexity while maintaining clean separation of concerns. This approach enables progressive enhancement, parallel development, and improved maintainability while keeping components modular and testable.

---

*Based on: UI Components Law Article 4*  
*Derives from: [MP017 Separation of Concerns](../../CH00_fundamental_principles/MP017_separation_of_concerns.qmd)*  
*Related to: [UI_R001 UI-Server-Defaults N-tuple](../rules/UI_R001_ui_server_defaults_ntuple.qmd), [UI_P003 Sidebar Filtering Only](UI_P003_sidebar_filtering_only.qmd)*
